title: G418 Kill curve protocol
description:
{"blocks":[{"key":"2ktvd","text":"Bodo saltans kill curve protocol using G418 (Gentamicin)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":56},{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c42k9","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"de206","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8vce","text":"Gentamycin exhibits toxicity toward both eukaryotic and prokaryotic cells by disrupting ribosome function, thereby blocking the elongation step in protein synthesis.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7lp73","text":"G418 is most commonly used as a selection agent for eukaryotic cells genetically engineered to express a neomycin resistance gene (NeoR), which is encoded by either transposon Tn601 (903) or Tn5.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9r4i4","text":"The resistant cells grow in medium containing G418, and may be used to establish stably transfected cell lines as all the non-resistant cells die due to G418 toxicity, typically within 6 – 14 days.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"53ta0","text":"To establish kill curve assay for  saltans various concentrations of G418 were tested. The concentration range from 1 to 50 μg/mL for selection of eukaryotic, and then higher concentrations can be used for maintaining stable transfected cell line.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":34,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7lcqj","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3j995","text":"Kill Curve Assay ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fhh2c","text":"Harvest the B. saltans cells from a culture that is at peak density (1 – 3.0 x 105) by centrifugation at 800 x g for 5 min., discard the supernatant (medium).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":12,"length":10},{"style":"sup","offset":81,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bq3b8","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"drjgr","text":"Replace the growth medium with fresh medium containing 0 – 50 μg/mL. For each concentration, test in triplicate.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"beql7","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3rr2q","text":"Using 6 wells plates, replace the medium in the wells every 3 – 4 days using fresh medium with the appropriate G418 concentration. Perform a daily visual inspection for evidence of toxicity, also cell count using hemocytometer.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1npqg","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"v5dj","text":"Note that the optimal dose of G418 for selection is the lowest one for which all cells have died after one week. A low dose is the concentration which has minimal effects on cells after 2 weeks of antibiotic selection. Conversely, a high dose is a concentration which is highly toxic to cells within 2-3 days of starting antibiotic selection.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7su6b","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3gl20","text":"5- The results indicate that a G418 concentration of  2 μg/mL kill the entire B. saltans population in 12 days which is considered enough time for selecting the resistant cells after transfection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":78,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"758kt","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7es83","text":"G418 Sensitivity for B.  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eehj8","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2dsos","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a2fja","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6ldh7","text":"Cell Selection ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"alnlh","text":"In all of our plasmids constructs we included the NeoR gene (Neomycine resistant gene) to select our transfected cells.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":50,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6tk1k","text":"Twenty-four hours after electroporation, the growth medium of transfected cells were supplemented with G418 antibiotic Solution (2 μg/mL) based on the kill curve results (above).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e25vv","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"67i3o","text":"3. Replace the G418-containing medium every 3 – 5 days and examine cells for visual toxicity. Most non-transfected (non-resistant) cells will die within 10- 12 days, leaving the transfected cells to expand.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":1,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ajl1n","text":"Once cells grow to high confluence, they may be maintained, or frozen as a polyclonal line or plated by limited dilution to select for single clones.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"flrbf","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":"","6_0":"","6_1":"","7_0":"","7_1":"","8_0":"","8_1":"","9_0":"","9_1":""},"colWidths":[null,null],"data":[["G418 (μg/ml)
